mechanism: The Godley identity forces any deficit reduction to manifest as either private sector surplus shrinkage (recessionary) or current account improvement (requires dollar depreciation). This arithmetic constraint explains why DOGE consolidation beyond modest amounts ($50-200B) would produce self-defeating GDP contraction.
regime_dependence: Accounting identity always holds. The behavioral prediction (private balance deterioration as primary adjustment channel) is regime-dependent: dollar depreciation could shift current account 1-2pp over 3-5 years.
